skill.name: gpt-image-2
skill.description: Generate and edit images with OpenAI GPT Image 2 (ChatGPT Images 2.0) on RunComfy. Documents GPT Image 2's strengths (embedded text, logos, multilingual typography, instruction precision), its 3 fixed sizes, edit-with-preservation language, and when to route to a sibling (Flux 2 / Nano Banana Pro / Seedream) instead. Calls `runcomfy run openai/gpt-image-2/text-to-image` or `/edit` through the local RunComfy CLI. Triggers on "gpt image 2", "gpt-image-2", "ChatGPT Images 2", "image 2", or any explicit ask to generate or edit with this model.
skill.summary: Generate and edit images with OpenAI GPT Image 2 on RunComfy. Includes expert prompting patterns, size constraints, and model selection guidance for optimal results.
skill.author: runcomfy-com

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 77 patterns across SKILL.md (58 shell backticks, 10 hardcoded URLs, 5 filesystem paths, 3 weak-crypto indicators, 1 reconnaissance). All findings evaluated as false positives: backticks contain markdown CLI documentation examples, URLs are legitimate RunComfy service endpoints, filesystem references document CLI config paths (~/.config/runcomfy/token.json), and crypto/reconnaissance patterns are misidentified text ("Exit codes" heading, jq pipe examples). No executable code, no prompt injection, no data exfiltration detected. Minor concerns: user prompts transmitted to RunComfy's external API, third-party CLI dependency, local token file storage.

low_findings.title: Third-Party Service Data Transmission
low_findings.description: User prompts and image URLs are transmitted to RunComfy's model API (model-api.runcomfy.net) for processing. This is the intended function but users should be aware their data leaves the local environment.

low_findings.title: Third-Party CLI Dependency
low_findings.description: Skill requires installation of @runcomfy/cli via npm. This introduces a supply chain dependency on an external package not controlled by the skill author.

low_findings.title: Local Token Storage
low_findings.description: RunComfy CLI stores authentication token in ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json. Documented as using mode 0600 permissions which provides adequate local protection.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Backtick Execution (58 instances — All False Positives)
dangerous_patterns.description: Backtick-wrapped shell commands detected throughout SKILL.md. All are markdown documentation examples showing CLI usage and sample commands. No executable code or injection vector exists.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hardcoded URLs (10 instances — All False Positives)
dangerous_patterns.description: RunComfy service URLs (runcomfy.com, model-api.runcomfy.net, docs.runcomfy.com) detected. These are legitimate documentation references and API endpoint descriptions for the service the skill wraps.

dangerous_patterns.title: Filesystem Path Patterns (5 instances — All False Positives)
dangerous_patterns.description: Path traversal sequences (../) and hidden file patterns (~/.config) detected. These are documentation of legitimate CLI behavior: --output-dir for saving generated images and ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json for token storage.

content.user_title: Generate Images with GPT Image 2 on RunComfy
content.value_statement: Create and edit images using OpenAI GPT Image 2 through the RunComfy cloud platform. This skill provides expert prompting patterns, size constraints, and model selection guidance so you get the best results on every generation.
actual_capabilities: Generate images from text prompts using GPT Image 2 on RunComfy
Edit existing images with natural language instructions while preserving specified elements
Render embedded text, logos, and multilingual typography in generated images
Route to the correct model based on user needs including Flux 2 and Nano Banana Pro
Handle async job submission with automatic polling and result download
Apply compositional cues like rule of thirds, close-up, and depth of field in prompts
limitations: Only three fixed image sizes are supported: 1024x1024, 1024x1536, and 1536x1024
Requires a RunComfy account and the RunComfy CLI installed locally
Generated images depend on RunComfy server availability and account billing status
Not suitable for extreme aspect ratios or photorealistic portraits

use_cases.title: E-Commerce Product Photography
use_cases.description: Generate product images with accurate label text, brand-safe lighting, and consistent styling across product lines using GPT Image 2's precise text rendering.

use_cases.title: Multilingual Brand Asset Creation
use_cases.description: Create signage, posters, and packaging mockups with accurate text rendering in multiple languages from a single source asset using the edit endpoint.
use_cases.target_user: Localization teams and global brand managers

use_cases.title: Iterative Image Refinement
use_cases.description: Edit images step by step, changing one attribute at a time while preserving composition, faces, and brand elements across multiple generations.

prompt_templates.title: Simple Text-to-Image Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image of [describe subject] in [describe setting] with [describe mood or lighting] using GPT Image 2 on RunComfy.

prompt_templates.title: Product Image with Embedded Text
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a product photo of [product] on [surface or background], the label reads "[exact text]" in [font style], [lighting description], e-commerce ready, neutral background.
prompt_templates.scenario: E-commerce product photography with accurate label text

prompt_templates.title: Image Edit with Preservation
prompt_templates.prompt: Edit this image using GPT Image 2: [describe specific change]. Keep [list elements to preserve] unchanged. Use [size] for output.
prompt_templates.scenario: Editing an existing image while keeping key elements intact

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Reference Image Composition
prompt_templates.prompt: Compose a new image using GPT Image 2 edit: subject from image 1 and background from image 2. Match the lighting of image 2. Keep the pose and face identity from image 1 unchanged.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced composition using multiple reference images for precise control

output_examples.input: Generate a product photo of a ceramic water bottle on warm linen with the text 'AQUA+' on the label
output_examples.output: High-resolution product image with accurate label text reading AQUA+, soft window lighting, subtle rim highlights, and neutral e-commerce background

output_examples.input: Edit this cafe photo: turn the background into a bright white studio sweep and add the headline 'OPEN STUDIO' in bold sans-serif, centered
output_examples.output: Edited image with clean white-to-soft-gray studio background, centered bold OPEN STUDIO headline text, preserved subject identity and composition

output_examples.input: Create a Tokyo cafe storefront at dusk with the sign reading the Japanese characters for coffee in kana
output_examples.output: Cinematic storefront image with warm interior glow, accurate Japanese text on wooden plaque, shallow depth of field, rule of thirds composition

best_practices: Quote all embedded text exactly as you want it to appear in the generated image for accurate text rendering
Change only one attribute per edit iteration such as lighting, background, pose, or text to maintain composition stability
Use compositional cues like rule of thirds, close-up, aerial view, or shallow depth of field directly in your prompts
anti_patterns: Do not combine conflicting instructions such as requesting no text while also specifying text on product labels
Avoid piling up multiple art styles like ukiyo-e plus watercolor plus cinematic in one prompt, which produces inconsistent results
Do not use extreme aspect ratios since only three fixed sizes are supported and unsupported ratios are auto-resized

faq.question: What is GPT Image 2 best at compared to other image models?
faq.answer: GPT Image 2 excels at embedded text rendering, logos, multilingual typography, and precise instruction following. It is ideal when accuracy matters more than artistic stylization.

faq.question: Do I need an OpenAI API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: No. This skill uses RunComfy's hosted Model API. You only need a RunComfy account and the RunComfy CLI installed locally.

faq.question: What image sizes are supported?
faq.answer: Three fixed sizes are supported: 1024x1024 for square, 1024x1536 for portrait, and 1536x1024 for landscape. The edit endpoint also supports auto to preserve the input aspect ratio.

faq.question: How does the edit endpoint preserve elements of the original image?
faq.answer: Use keep statements in your prompt, such as keep the person's pose and face identity unchanged. The model holds composition stable when you change only one attribute at a time.

faq.question: Is my prompt data sent to external servers?
faq.answer: Yes. Prompts are sent over HTTPS to RunComfy's model API at model-api.runcomfy.net. The CLI does not shell-expand prompts. Review RunComfy's privacy policy for details on data handling.

faq.question: How do I cancel a running image generation?
faq.answer: Press Ctrl-C while the CLI is polling for results. This sends a cancel request to the API so you are not billed for unused GPU time.
